content:
From var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args) to Production-Grade Pipeline

10 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Program.cs is the most important file in your ASP.NET Core application. It's the composition root where services are registered, middleware is ordered, and the entire request pipeline is defined. Yet most developers copy-paste from templates without understanding what each line actually does.
In .NET 10, Program.cs has evolved significantly. The WebApplication builder pattern introduced in .NET 6 is now mature, with new APIs for OpenAPI 3.1, built-in rate limiting, output caching, and streamlined authentication. This article is the definitive reference — every builder.Services.Add... call, every app.Use... middleware, and the exact order that prevents security vulnerabilities.
The Anatomy of Program.cs
A production Program.cs has two distinct phases:
// PHASE 1: Service Registration (Composition Root)
var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);
builder.Services.Add... // Everything your app needs to run
// PHASE 2: Middleware Pipeline (Request Processing)
var app = builder.Build();
app.Use... // Everything that processes HTTP…
